justifications.default: The benchmark aligns with the general cross-industry baseline defined in the Gartner I&O Maturity Index (2026). Operational processes in these non-regulated domains are expected to operate at Level 4 (Managed and Sustainable), characterized by documented procedures and standardized execution across business units.
justifications.T2: Sourced from the Spanish Royal Decree 311/2022 (Esquema Nacional de Seguridad - ENS) high-category control matrix and NIS2 Article 21. Standard security measures for IT/OT convergence demand strict environment segregation, network access controls, and formalized platform governance, contractually raising the baseline to Level 4 (Managed and Sustainable) to prevent unauthorized control-plane lateral movement.
justifications.T5: Calibrated against EU NIS2 Directive 2022/2555 requirements for essential service continuity. Transmission System Operators (TSOs) are operationally mandated to maintain high-availability platforms with multi-site geographic redundancy, sub-hour Recovery Time Objectives (RTO), and continuous replication pipelines, mapping to a Level 4.2 (Predictable) operational state.
justifications.T6: Directly mapped to national critical infrastructure protection laws (Ley PIC) and the high-category control matrix of ENS. Requires automated, 24/7 security operations center (SOC) ingestion, strict perimeter zoning (IT/OT airgapping), and continuous cryptographic signing of control-plane payloads, pushing the benchmark to Level 4.5 (Optimized Threat Resilience).
justifications.T8: Derived from Gartner Utility IT Governance Best Practices (2026). The baseline is calibrated to Level 4.0 to reflect the mandatory compliance reporting and regulated asset lifecycle management typical of transmission system operators, requiring formalized cost-attribution and multi-cloud governance frameworks.
